pi_items.source: CAS Common Chemistry and multiple chemical databases confirm CAS 7697-37-2 is Nitric Acid
CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4044 lists 'NITRIC ACID, RED FUMING' as valid chemical entry
CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response section at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4044 recommends 'Cover with DRY earth, DRY sand or other non-combustible material' - NO neutralizers mentioned
CAS Common Chemistry and multiple chemical databases confirm CAS 1310-73-2 is Sodium Hydroxide
CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1499 lists 'SODIUM HYDROXIDE SOLUTION' as valid chemical entry

CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response section at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/1499 recommends 'Absorb or cover with dry earth, sand or other non-combustible material' - NO neutralizers mentioned. Vinegar appears only in First Aid section for skin contact, NOT in Non-Fire Response
CAS Common Chemistry and multiple chemical databases confirm CAS 7664-39-3 is Hydrofluoric Acid
CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2013 lists 'HYDROFLUORIC ACID, SOLUTION' as valid chemical entry

CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response section at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2013 recommends 'Cover with DRY earth, DRY sand or other non-combustible material' - NO neutralizers mentioned. Calcium gluconate gel is mentioned only in First Aid section, not as a Non-Fire Response neutralizer
CAS Common Chemistry and multiple chemical databases confirm CAS 302-01-2 is Hydrazine
CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/5019 lists 'HYDRAZINE, ANHYDROUS' as valid chemical entry

CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response section at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/5019 recommends 'Absorb with earth, sand or other non-combustible material. For hydrazine, absorb with DRY sand or inert absorbent (vermiculite or absorbent pads)' - NO neutralizers mentioned
CAS Common Chemistry and multiple chemical databases confirm CAS 108-88-3 is Toluene
CAMEO Chemicals at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654 lists 'TOLUENE' as valid chemical entry

CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response section at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/4654 states 'Absorb or cover with dry earth, sand or other non-combustible material' - no specific chemical neutralizers recommended
Path specified in task.json metadata; verified to be created by export_result.sh hook

privileged_info_summary: The task requires agents to research five chemicals (Nitric Acid Red Fuming CAS 7697-37-2, Sodium Hydroxide Solution CAS 1310-73-2, Hydrofluoric Acid CAS 7664-39-3, Hydrazine Anhydrous CAS 302-01-2, Toluene CAS 108-88-3) on the CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/). All five chemicals have verified entries on CAMEO Chemicals with real, public data. According to CAMEO Chemicals Non-Fire Response sections: Nitric Acid Red Fuming recommends covering with dry earth/sand only, NOT lime/soda ash/bicarbonate; Sodium Hydroxide Solution recommends absorption with dry earth/sand only, NOT vinegar/acetic acid (which appears only in First Aid section); Hydrofluoric Acid recommends covering with dry earth/sand only, NOT calcium/lime/hydroxide (calcium gluconate appears only in First Aid); Hydrazine Anhydrous recommends absorption with dry sand or inert absorbent only, NOT hypochlorite/acid/HTH; Toluene correctly requires no specific neutralizers, only absorption with inert materials. The task metadata keywords do NOT match CAMEO Chemicals actual Non-Fire Response recommendations for 4 out of 5 chemicals.
